nodes.label: AI as creative partner maintaining writer's voice
nodes.label_negative: AI as generic voice diluting idiosyncratic style
nodes.grounding_span: it would also feel nice to feel like I have more of a workshop partner who can identify my stylistic goals

nodes.label: AI as gap-bridge for fragmented memories
nodes.label_negative: AI unable to change fundamental non-narrative thinking
nodes.grounding_span: AI could certainly help me identify those gaps or consider other structures

nodes.label: AI as validator of creative work
nodes.label_negative: AI as overly validating without realistic market feedback
nodes.grounding_span: I find AI can be too validating (wow! this is great! it should be published right now!)

nodes.label: AI as tool for identifying poetic obsessions and themes
nodes.grounding_span: AI could help me identify 'poetic obsessions' or recurring themes within them

nodes.label: AI as market-aware publishing assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI lacking knowledge of successful books and contest trends
nodes.grounding_span: if it had more knowledge of successful books and could identify the threads through them

nodes.label: Authentic creative expression
nodes.grounding_span: I'm driving the creative decisions, and I wouldn't want it any other way

nodes.label: Creative productivity and progress
nodes.grounding_span: get the work polished enough to submit to agents and publishers

nodes.label: Market success and recognition
nodes.grounding_span: help me get a better grasp on what's being looked for in recent contests and publishing deals

nodes.label: Desire for AI as stylistic partner
nodes.grounding_span: it would also feel nice to feel like I have more of a workshop partner who can identify my stylistic goals

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's generic output
nodes.grounding_span: when it gives me ideas, they're often the most pared-down, simple version of what I'm trying to say

nodes.label: Skepticism about AI's over-validation
nodes.grounding_span: I find AI can be too validating (wow! this is great! it should be published right now!)

nodes.label: Openness to AI for market insights
nodes.grounding_span: if it had more knowledge of successful books and could identify the threads through them

nodes.label: Fragmented associative thinking — prefers non-linear, image-based connections
nodes.grounding_span: I don't really think in terms of narrative

nodes.label: Reflective iterative experimentation — tests tools in bounded ways before committing
nodes.grounding_span: I've tried it a little bit to help me bridge gaps, and I've found it helpful
